condition: The number K of cross-fitting folds in def:feasible-erm is a fixed finite integer independent of n, and the deterministic fold partition is balanced as stated there.
reason: The pooled cross-fit empirical-process reduction uses sum_k (n_k/n)n_k^{-1/2}=O(n^{-1/2}); if K grew with n this factor would be O((K/n)^{1/2}).
standard_or_novel: standard: fixed-K cross-fitting, as in Chernozhukov et al. (2018) DML
not_crux: This is an estimator-side bookkeeping condition for the fold reduction; it does not assert any regret rate, drift cancellation, overlap stability, or nuisance learning improvement.
